fields.Name: XGBoost
fields.Description: Gradient-boosted decision-tree ensemble that builds trees sequentially to correct prior errors. Reach for it as a strong default on tabular classification and regression problems where accuracy matters; the tradeoff is more hyperparameters to tune and slower training than linear models.

fields.Name: LightGBM
fields.Description: Histogram-based gradient-boosting framework that grows trees leaf-wise for speed and memory efficiency. Reach for it over XGBoost on large datasets where training time matters; the tradeoff is that leaf-wise growth can overfit smaller datasets without careful regularization.

fields.Name: Logistic Regression
fields.Description: Linear model that estimates class probabilities via the logistic function. Reach for it as a fast, interpretable baseline for classification when relationships are roughly linear; the tradeoff is limited capacity to capture nonlinear interactions without manual feature engineering.

fields.Name: Random Forest
fields.Description: Ensemble of decision trees trained on bootstrapped samples with randomized feature splits, averaging their predictions. Reach for it as a robust, low-tuning default that resists overfitting; the tradeoff is larger models and weaker extrapolation than boosted trees on some problems.

fields.Name: Ridge Regression
fields.Description: Linear regression with L2 regularization that shrinks coefficients to reduce variance. Reach for it on regression problems with many correlated features where ordinary least squares overfits; the tradeoff is that it only models linear relationships and never zeroes out features.

fields.Name: Multilayer Perceptron
fields.Description: Feedforward neural network with one or more hidden layers that learns nonlinear feature representations. Reach for it when complex nonlinear patterns dominate and you have enough data; the tradeoff is sensitivity to scaling and tuning, longer training, and lower interpretability.
